content.text:
*long pause, voice gets quieter*

To her, I guess. 

To... I don't know. The last few months were hard. She was sick for a while and it was... it wasn't a good death, if that's a thing. And I was there for a lot of it and my brother and sister weren't and I'm angry about that, actually, I'm pretty angry about a lot of it.

*catches self*

See, this is the thing. When I start going there it just opens up and it's not... it's not clean. It's not just sad. It's complicated and messy and I don't know what to do with all the different parts of it.

*quieter*

She and I had a complicated relationship. It wasn't like losing your best friend. It was... different. And I don't know if I'm allowed to grieve something complicated.
